mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an impending economic contraction, triggering downward revisions to corporate earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power for earnings revision shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized broad-based economic expansion, which supports cyclical corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over three consecutive rolling quarters, or the correlation between the proxy spread and SPX returns drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds the Federal Reserve implicit target, real interest rates rise and purchasing power erodes, forcing equity risk premiums higher and depressing forward market valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite CPI exceeding 260 and rising policy rates, or the interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a regression of forward returns on the inflation-rate spread.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth serves as a direct proxy for aggregate corporate revenue trends, mechanically translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a six-month horizon as earnings catch up to macroeconomic expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in GDP contraction quarters are not significantly lower than expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls trigger immediate algorithmic risk-off positioning and downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite sharp job losses, or the signal shows no predictive power for short-term volatility spikes.

mechanism_narrative: High auto sales volumes combined with elevated housing starts indicate robust consumer credit demand and wealth effects, which support cyclical equity valuations and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over three consecutive rolling quarters, or the correlation between the proxy spread and SPX returns drops below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, industrial production rebounds drive short-term equity momentum as supply chain bottlenecks clear and capacity utilization rises, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in production rebound periods are not significantly higher than baseline.
